African travel guide and booking directory for regions, countries, attractions, stays, dining, and trusted partner booking paths. Directory-first planning with partner booking links for Africa travel.

Discover Africa

Explore Africa through regions first, then book with confidence.

Trek Africa Guide helps travelers understand the shape of an Africa trip before they commit. Start with East, West, Southern, or Northern Africa, compare countries, open a destination, and then continue to trusted booking partners for tours, stays, and dining.

Explore Regions Browse Listings

Africa in 2026

A continent of different travel rhythms, not one single market.

Africa is rebounding strongly, but travelers still need clear guidance. North Africa is aviation-led and culture-rich, East Africa remains the strongest safari-and-primate gateway, Southern Africa combines wilderness with polished urban circuits, and West Africa shines for heritage, coastlines, music, and diaspora travel.

How Trek Africa Guide works

  • Start with a region and compare the countries that welcome visitors.
  • Open a country page to see attractions, tour operators, stays, and restaurants in one place.
  • Use listing pages styled like modern travel marketplaces, then continue to an external booking partner when ready.

Featured Regions

The four major entry points for planning an Africa trip.

Each region page leads into country-level guidance and then into bookable attractions, stays, and restaurants.

countries

East Africa

East Africa remains one of the continent’s highest-demand regions for safari, gorilla trekking, coast add-ons, and premium conservation travel. Kenya and Tanzania lead the classic plains circuits, while Uganda and Rwanda anchor primate-led journeys.
countries

West Africa

West Africa is driven by heritage journeys, winter-sun escapes, coastal cities, and community-grounded experiences. Ghana and Senegal are the most polished starting points, while Benin, Sierra Leone, and Cabo Verde broaden the offer considerably.
countries

Southern Africa

Southern Africa is one of the continent’s most versatile travel regions. South Africa acts as the gateway, Botswana and Namibia deliver high-value wilderness and desert landscapes, and Zimbabwe and Zambia deepen the safari-and-Zambezi story.
countries

Northern Africa

Northern Africa is Africa’s volume engine for tourism, driven by Morocco, Egypt, Tunisia, and a growing wave of renewed interest in Algeria. Travelers come for cities, heritage, desert camps, Mediterranean coastlines, and food-led travel.

Featured Attractions

Open individual attraction pages built like a modern travel marketplace.

Listing cards lead to full independent landing pages with galleries, planning notes, nearby stays, dining, and external booking buttons.

Uganda

Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Mountain gorilla trekking in one of Africa’s most powerful forest landscapes.

★ 4.9 (842) From $700
Southwestern Uganda
View Details

Kenya

Maasai Mara

Kenya’s classic safari icon for migration drama, predators, and broad savannah horizons.

★ 4.9 (1,384) From $95
Southwestern Kenya
View Details

Tanzania

Serengeti National Park

Vast migration landscapes, apex predators, and one of Africa’s strongest safari names.

★ 4.9 (1,196) From $110
Northern Tanzania
View Details

South Africa

Cape Town

A design-led city with ocean views, wine access, and easy day trips.

★ 4.8 (1,675) From $20
Western Cape
View Details

Zimbabwe

Victoria Falls

The classic waterfall gateway for rainforest walks, river activities, and onward safari routing.

★ 4.7 (882) From $28
Zimbabwe
View Details

Morocco

Marrakech & Atlas Gateway

A flexible route of medina culture, gardens, riads, and day trips into the Atlas foothills.

★ 4.7 (1,222) From $16
Morocco
View Details

Featured Stays

Accommodation shortlists tied to real attractions.

Every stay is linked to a country, region, and nearby attraction so travelers can judge route logic before they leave the site.

Uganda

Bwindi Impenetrable Lodge & Retreat

A comfortable base positioned for travelers visiting Bwindi Impenetrable National Park, with easy early-start logistics and a travel-friendly atmosphere.

Dining recommendations that add local flavor to the trip.

Restaurants sit alongside attractions and stays so destination pages feel practical rather than brochure-like.

Affiliate notice: booking buttons redirect to external partner pages for the selected listing. Always verify live availability, rates, and final booking terms on the partner side.
